claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in the 20-week extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 16 and in the 20-week extension period; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 16 and in the 20-week extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start: **How many children are you enquiring about, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm interested in finding out if I can claim Child Benefit. 

I have one child - Alex, who is 16 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex.

Since Alex is 16, their eligibility depends on their current situation. **Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** 

For example, this would include:
- A-levels or equivalent qualifications
- Scottish Highers
- NVQs up to Level 3
- A traineeship in England

Or has Alex left education/training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex has recently left school actually. They finished their course and have been signed up with the local careers service to help figure out what to do next - you know, get some guidance on their options.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that helpful information.

Since Alex is 16 and has left school, I need to clarify a few things. Let me check the specific rules around this situation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
This is very helpful! Based on the information you've provided, I have some good news.

According to the rules, you can apply for Child Benefit to continue for **20 weeks** if a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training and registers with either:
- A government-sponsored careers service
- The armed services

Since Alex is 16, has left school, and is registered with the local careers service, this appears to meet the criteria for the 20-week extension.

I just need to confirm a couple more things:

1. **Do you live in the UK?**
2. **Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester. And yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. That's helpful.

Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is Alex currently working 24 hours or more per week, or receiving any benefits in their own right (such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't working that much - just helps out occasionally. And no, they're not receiving any benefits themselves.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary:

**For Alex (age 16):** ✅ Eligible

Because Alex is 16, has left school, and is registered with a local careers service (which counts as a government-sponsored careers service), you qualify for Child Benefit to continue for a **20-week extension period**.

The key factors supporting your eligibility are:
- You live in the UK
- Alex lives with you, so you're responsible for them
- Alex is registered with a careers service after leaving education
- Alex is not working 24+ hours per week
- Alex is not receiving benefits in their own right

**Important to note:** This eligibility is for a 20-week period. If Alex starts approved education or training during this time (such as A-levels, NVQs up to Level 3, or similar), you may be able to continue receiving Child Benefit until they turn 20. If Alex doesn't return to approved education or training, Child Benefit will stop after the 20 weeks.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 16 years old, has left education, and is registered with a local careers service (a government-sponsored careers service). According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you can apply for Child Benefit to continue for 20 weeks if a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training and registers with either a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services. Alex meets this criterion. Additionally: (1) The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), (2) Alex lives with the claimant, so the claimant is responsible for Alex, (3) Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week, and (4) Alex is not receiving benefits in their own right. All eligibility conditions are satisfied for the 20-week extension period.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK and is responsible for Alex, who lives with them. Alex is 16, has left school, and is registered with a government-sponsored careers service. This qualifies the claimant for Child Benefit to continue for a 20-week period. Alex does not have any disqualifying factors such as working 24+ hours per week or receiving benefits in their own right.

Alex meets this criterion. Additionally: (1) The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), (2) Alex lives with the claimant, so the claimant is responsible for Alex, (3) Alex is not working 24 hours or more per week, and (4) Alex is not receiving benefits in their own right. All eligibility conditions are satisfied for the 20-week extension period.

This qualifies the claimant for Child Benefit to continue for a 20-week period. Alex does not have any disqualifying factors such as working 24+ hours per week or receiving benefits in their own right.
